acceptanceCriteria: normalizeText collapses all whitespace runs to a single space and trims ends (e.g. "  a\n\n b\t c " → "a b c")
normalizeText returns empty string for empty or whitespace-only input
wordCount returns 0 for empty or whitespace-only input
wordCount returns the number of whitespace-separated tokens on normalized text (e.g. "a  b\nc" → 3)
truncateWords returns the first maxWords words joined by single spaces, operating on normalized text
truncateWords appends … (U+2026) only when words were actually dropped
truncateWords returns empty string when maxWords <= 0
truncateWords returns the normalized text unchanged (no ellipsis) when word count ≤ maxWords
The returned text from truncateWords, minus a trailing …, never exceeds maxWords words
All three functions are exported from a barrel index at apps/web/src/lib/text-utilities/index.ts
npm run build:web passes with no errors
npm run test passes with all new tests green
outOfScope: Wiring these utilities into the synthesis/dispatch pipeline
SSML-aware tokenization or language-specific word segmentation
Refactoring or reusing the existing chunk-text utility
Any integration beyond standalone functions and their tests
Performance optimization beyond reasonable O(n) behavior
problemStatement: Synthesis features need clean, predictable text inputs, but every developer who touches the synthesis path currently has to invent (or copy-paste) their own whitespace normalization, word counting, and word truncation logic. There is no shared, tested module for these fundamental text-prep operations, which leads to inconsistent behavior across synthesis entry points and wasted effort re-solving the same micro-problems.
userValue: Developers building synthesis features get three small, reliable, well-tested utilities they can reach for immediately — no reinventing normalization, no yak-shaving over "what counts as a word," no off-by-one ellipsis bugs. This keeps synthesis work focused on synthesis, not on text munging.
